prompt: Is trying to train for strength and hypertrophy at the same time basically useless for recreational intermediate lifters? Does it make sense to mix goals by body part or day?
expected_output: Greg said that framing is wrong: concurrent strength and hypertrophy work is usually effective for most non-elite lifters. He suggested two practical templates: (1) mostly hypertrophy work while sprinkling in occasional heavy singles around ~90-92% to keep high-load skill sharp, or (2) block-style programming with about 75% of blocks focused on hypertrophy and every fourth block more strength-specific. He noted that near-max, low-volume peaking work can temporarily limit hypertrophy if done constantly, but you only need short doses of it (e.g., a few weeks every 4-6 months for many lifters). For long-term strength, he emphasized that adding muscle is foundational, so hypertrophy training should be a major part of the year. Bottom line: build size most of the time, keep some heavy practice in, and both outcomes can improve together.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on concurrent strength and hypertrophy training
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer includes actionable practical recommendations consistent with the hosts' advice
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
